name: fim-inline
description: High-performance VS Code inline completion using DeepSeek V4 FIM API. Full-file context + cross-file preamble for extreme KV-cache hit rates.

FIM.provider.description: Provider preset. Use 'deepseek' for the official DeepSeek hosted API; use 'custom' for an OpenAI-compatible FIM endpoint with prompt/suffix fields.

FIM.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the FIM API. For official FIM use `https://api.deepseek.com/beta`. When using a custom provider, change if needed.

FIM.completionsPath.description: Path appended to `baseUrl` for the completion request.

FIM.model.description: Model name sent to the FIM endpoint. DeepSeek FIM Beta supports deepseek-v4-pro with 1M token context window.

FIM.apiKeyEnvVar.description: Environment variable used as fallback when no API key is stored in VS Code SecretStorage.

FIM.maxTokens.description: Maximum generated tokens for each inline completion. The FIM Beta API supports up to 4K tokens.

FIM.temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0.0 gives deterministic completions (recommended for code).

FIM.withSuffix.description: Send suffix text after the cursor. DeepSeek FIM supports true Fill-In-the-Middle with both prefix and suffix. Turn off for prefix-only APIs.

FIM.requestTimeoutMs.description: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds. Increased from the old default to accommodate larger full-file prompts.

FIM.debounceMs.description: Delay before making the request. VS Code usually cancels older requests while typing.

FIM.stop.description: Optional stop strings sent to the completion endpoint.

FIM.logRequests.description: Log request metadata to the FIM output channel. Does not log API keys. Enables visibility into prompt/suffix sizes, TTFB, and cache hits.

FIM.streamEnabled.description: Enable progressive streaming: show completions as soon as the first chunk of tokens arrives, then continue fetching more in the background for the next keystroke.

FIM.streamTokens.description: How many tokens to collect before showing the first inline completion chunk. Lower values make completions appear faster. The rest of the stream is cached for the next keystroke.

FIM.streamCacheTtlMs.description: How long (in ms) to keep cached stream continuations. When you type again within this window, the next tokens are served from cache without an API call.

FIM.minPrefixChars.description: Do not request completions until at least this many non-whitespace prefix characters exist before the cursor.

FIM.preambleEnabled.description: Include cross-file project context (other source files) as a preamble before the current file's prefix. The preamble is loaded once and reused across all keystrokes — it is always at the same token positions, achieving 100% server-side KV-cache hit for the preamble portion. Filtered by .gitignore and files.exclude.

FIM.preambleMaxFiles.description: Maximum number of other project files to include in the cross-file preamble.

FIM.preambleMaxChars.description: Soft cap on total characters for the preamble (before adding the current file). DeepSeek V4 supports 1M tokens (~4M chars), so 500K chars leaves ample room for the current file and completion.

FIM.preambleIncludePatterns.description: Additional glob patterns for files to INCLUDE in the preamble. When empty, all files matching source-code extensions are considered. Example: ["src/**/*.ts", "lib/**/*.py"].

FIM.preambleExcludePatterns.description: Glob patterns for files to EXCLUDE from the preamble. Defaults exclude common build artifacts, lockfiles, and dependency directories. Merged with VS Code's files.exclude and .gitignore settings.
